questions.prompt: Why invest in docs / KB early in a SaaS lifecycle?

choices.text: Docs deflect support volume — customers Google their question, find an article, get unblocked without writing a ticket. Reduces support staffing needs as you scale; same content helps everyone. Investment in docs has compounding return: write once, deflects forever. At scale, a good KB can deflect 30-50% of potential tickets, dramatically reducing per-customer support cost. Beyond cost: faster customer resolution (seconds via search vs hours waiting for support reply); higher self-serve activation (users discover features in docs); easier onboarding for new hires (read the docs your customers read). Tools (Mintlify, ReadMe, Intercom Articles, custom Next.js docs) make this manageable. Pair with in-app help (tooltips, search widget from app), and you've moved a lot of support friction to deflection. Counterintuitive ROI: docs feel like 'work for later' but pay back continuously as customer count grows. Start docs early; iterate as you learn what customers actually ask.

questions.explanation: Docs deflect 30-50% of support tickets; pay back continuously. Faster customer resolution; lower support cost.

questions.prompt: Customer success wants to know which accounts are at-risk. How?

choices.text: Build a health score combining: engagement (DAU/MAU trend — falling = bad), feature adoption (% of features used, especially key ones), commercial signals (renewal date approaching, payment failures, plan downgrades), support sentiment (CSAT, ticket volume — high volume = pain), executive sponsor signals (champion left? main user gone?). Weight each; score 0-100; red/yellow/green buckets nightly. CS team works from a 'red accounts dashboard' — proactive outreach to investigate, offer help, identify and address root causes BEFORE the customer churns. Tools (Gainsight, Vitally, ChurnZero, Catalyst) are CS-specific platforms for this. Without health scoring, CS works reactively (only on accounts who complain); proactive intervention catches accounts that drift silently. Industry benchmark: proactive CS reduces churn 10-30% on accounts they touch. Standard practice for any SaaS with revenue per customer high enough to justify human CS attention (typically $5K+ ACV).

questions.explanation: Health score (engagement + adoption + commercial + sentiment). Tools: Gainsight, Vitally, ChurnZero.
